title: Protein Family Retrieval: Design the Similarity Kernel
strategies: raw squared-Euclidean nearest-neighbour on the descriptors as given: retrieval is dominated by the per-protein abundance/length factor and the loud nuisance channels, reproducing the evaluator's weak reference (~0.1)

L1-normalize each descriptor to a composition (removes the abundance factor) then divide each channel by its across-batch std (equalizes nuisance vs informative scale), cosine kNN -- but skip centering, so the constant per-channel offsets still tilt the cosine: a little above baseline

full standardized kernel: composition-normalize + per-channel z-score (center out the baseline offsets AND equalize scale) + cosine kNN, so the quiet informative channels dominate the geometry and same-fold proteins cluster -- large mAP gain with headroom left

channel-reweighted / whitened kernels: on top of standardization, upweight channels with the most between-cluster (multimodal) structure or decorrelate channels, pushing mAP further toward the (unreachable) ceiling
